title: Chunky Leg Side Table
summary: A small two-tier side table on four chunky 2x6 legs, with 1x12 shelf boards framed top and bottom by 1x3/1x2 frames measured and cut to fit the assembled base.
description:
Four short 2x6 legs give this side table real visual heft for its small footprint — chunky legs on a small table read very differently than the same legs on a larger piece, and that contrast is the whole design idea. The four 1x12 boards form the top shelf, laid edge to edge across the leg assembly.

**Both the top and bottom shelf frames are measured and cut to fit your own assembled legs** rather than built to a fixed dimension — the plan explicitly calls out several pieces as "measure and cut to fit" — since leg placement and board thickness both shift the frame size slightly. Build the top shelf frame from 1x3 and the bottom shelf frame from 1x2, matching a common table design language where the upper frame is meaningfully sturdier than the lower one.

Use a jigsaw for any final trimming needed to get the frames to seat cleanly against the legs — this is a small enough piece that hand-fitting each frame member is faster than trying to nail an exact cut list number.

steps.title: Build the top shelf frame
steps.body: Measure your assembled legs and cut the 1x3 top frame pieces to fit, then assemble.

steps.title: Build the bottom shelf frame
steps.body: Measure and cut the 1x2 bottom frame pieces to fit, then assemble.

steps.title: Attach the shelf boards
steps.body: Attach the 1x12 shelf boards to the top frame.

steps.title: Assemble the table
steps.body: Attach the top and bottom frame assemblies to the four legs.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand with 120 and 150 grit sandpaper and apply paint or stain.

unresolvedImages.alt: Chunky leg side table with two tiers
